title: SieveRuntime trait is public but evaluator never calls it
description: evaluate() hardcodes DefaultRuntime internally; the evaluator dispatch in eval_stmt has no SieveRuntime parameter. Users who implement SieveRuntime can never pass it to anything — the public evaluate() ignores it. The doc comment on evaluate() acknowledges this: 'once the evaluator layer is wired to accept a runtime (planned follow-up).' A public trait that callers cannot use is dead scaffolding that will generate 'how do I use SieveRuntime?' issues. Either wire it into the evaluator now, or remove it from the public API until it is.
